examples.title: Recursive Binary Search in C
examples.description: Standard recursive binary search implementation with proper overflow prevention
examples.explanation: The function takes array, low index, high index, and target. It calculates mid safely, compares with target, and recursively searches left or right half. Returns index if found, -1 otherwise.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(log n) due to recursive call stack

examples.title: Recursive Binary Search in Python
examples.description: Python implementation showing clean recursive binary search
examples.explanation: Python version demonstrates same algorithm with Python syntax. Uses integer division (//) for mid calculation. Returns -1 for not found, index when found.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(log n) due to recursion depth

examples.title: Recursive Binary Search in Java
examples.description: Java implementation with proper class structure and method
examples.explanation: Java implementation follows similar logic to C. The recursive method returns the index of the target or -1. Main method demonstrates usage with sample array.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(log n) due to recursive call stack

examples.title: Binary Search Recursion Algorithm (Generic)
examples.description: Pseudocode showing the generic recursive binary search algorithm
examples.code:
FUNCTION BinarySearch(arr, low, high, target)
BEGIN
    // Base Case 1: Element not found
    IF low > high THEN
        RETURN -1
    END IF
    
    // Calculate middle index (prevents overflow)
    mid = low + (high - low) / 2
    
    // Base Case 2: Element found
    IF arr[mid] == target THEN
        RETURN mid
    END IF
    
    // Recursive Case 1: Search left half
    IF arr[mid] > target THEN
        RETURN BinarySearch(arr, low, mid - 1, target)
    END IF
    
    // Recursive Case 2: Search right half
    RETURN BinarySearch(arr, mid + 1, high, target)
END FUNCTION

// Main program
INPUT arr[0..n-1]  // Sorted array
INPUT target       // Element to search

result = BinarySearch(arr, 0, n-1, target)

IF result == -1 THEN
    PRINT "Element not found"
ELSE
    PRINT "Element found at index " + result
END IF
examples.explanation: This pseudocode clearly shows the algorithm structure: two base cases (not found, found) and two recursive cases (search left or right half). The mid calculation prevents integer overflow in languages with fixed-size integers.
